cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Richard could have gone to the basketball court. The key is to consider all the information given and see when he was available. First, we know that Richard woke up at 5am. So before that, he was asleep, so he couldn't have gone to the court before 5am. Then, Elizabeth saw him taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 5am to 7am. That means during that time, he was in Italy, so he couldn't have been at the basketball court then either. Next, Lisa saw him fixing a computer at the electronic store from 3pm to 7pm. So during those four hours, he was busy at the store. After that, Leslie saw him reading at the library from 7pm to 8pm. The basketball court was closed after 8pm, so he had to be there before that. Putting this together, let's look at the timeline: - Before 5am: Asleep - 5am - 7am: In Italy with Elizabeth - 7am - 3pm: What was he doing here? Not mentioned, so maybe free time - 3pm - 7pm: At the electronic store with Lisa - 7pm - 8pm: At the library with Leslie - After 8pm: Court is closed So the only time window where he's not accounted for is between 7am and 3pm. During that time, there's no mention of him doing anything else. Therefore, he could have gone to the basketball court then. Let me check the options again. Option D is 7am to 3pm. Let me make sure there's no other conflicting info. The court is closed after 8pm, but he was at the library from 7-8pm, so he couldn't have gone after 3pm
